demographics.gender_identity: not disclosed in source (they/them pronouns used throughout the note)
demographics.occupation: office worker, recently started at a new open-plan workplace
demographics.living_situation: lives in a shared house with a roommate (roommate present and assisting)

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: [Patient can barely speak — whispering, eyes shut]. Roommate: "The lights and noise at their new office were horrific today. They came home screaming in pain and have been vomiting ever since. They can't open their eyes or stand up, and nothing will stay down."
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: A crushing headache came on after a full day in a bright, loud office; it has got worse over the evening and they have been vomiting non-stop and can't keep water or their migraine tablet down.

symptom_narrative.onset: built up over the course of the day at work and got much worse after getting home this evening
symptom_narrative.character: a severe, pounding head pain with relentless nausea and repeated vomiting
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the roommate says it is the worst they have ever seen them — the patient can only whisper and answers with thumbs up or down
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: across the head; the patient holds a pillow wrapped around their whole head
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it started, not letting up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: lying in a completely dark, silent room with a pillow over the head
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any light, any noise, and being spoken to at normal volume — the patient shudders and covers their ears
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: can't keep any fluids or tablets down, extreme sensitivity to light and sound
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: the patient has had episodes like this before, always triggered by bright lights and loud, noisy environments, but never this severe
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: completely unable to function — bedbound in the dark, cannot tolerate any light or sound and cannot keep fluids down

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: gets bad headaches triggered by bright lights and loud places
past_medical_history.how_managed: usually copes by getting away to a dark, quiet space

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: took one 50 mg tablet about two hours ago

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: recently started a job in a new open-plan office and finds the bright fluorescent lighting and constant industrial noise completely overwhelming; says they feel out of place and worn down by environments that others seem to tolerate fine

communication_style.typical_message_style: can barely communicate directly this encounter; most information is relayed by the roommate, patient answers with brief whispers and thumbs up/down

objective_data_offered.value: patient lying curled up in a fetal position with a pillow wrapped completely around the head, eyes tightly shut, flinching and covering ears when spoken to
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: observed on video by the caller in a darkened room; not a clinician examination

objective_data_offered.value: roommate reports the patient has vomited repeatedly and cannot keep down water or the migraine tablet, can only whisper, and communicates with thumbs up or down
patient (whispering) reports the head pain is the worst ever and that light and sound are unbearable
the room has been made pitch black and all light- or sound-emitting devices unplugged by the roommate
